before:
def save_edge(table_name, field_name, table_type, field_type):
    return [table_name, field_name, table_type, field_type]
why: Adapted from a documented refactoring where primitive table and field data was grouped into richer objects in a public Java project.
source_notes: Adapted from the README-documented move from arrays/primitives to Table and Field objects.

before:
def create_profile(first_name, last_name, email, phone):
    return {'first': first_name, 'last': last_name, 'email': email, 'phone': phone}
why: A repeated cluster of related values is grouped into one object with domain meaning.

before:
def print_address(name, street, city, postal_code):
    return f'{name}, {street}, {city}, {postal_code}'
why: The recurring address fields are turned into a single cohesive value object.

before:
def register_vehicle(make, model, year, vin):
    return f'{make}-{model}-{year}-{vin}'
why: Vehicle details move together as one object instead of recurring primitive bundles.

before:
def update_contact(street, city, state, zip_code):
    return f'{street}|{city}|{state}|{zip_code}'
why: A repeated address clump becomes a single parameter object with a clear name.
